issues: News report served from 7.8h stale cache — acceptable for macro but could miss intraday catalysts

Fundamentals hash rate data internally contradictory (current 1045 EH/s vs 30d avg 941 EH/s with identical 7d/30d averages and conflicting trend signals) — analyst flagged it but system should auto-reject contradictory data
Settlement volume 71% below 30d average flagged but not resolved — weekend effect vs genuine distribution signal left ambiguous
1d OBV slope deeply negative while vol_trend says 'up' — contradictory indicators passed through without reconciliation
Aggressive analyst pushed for 8-9/10 conviction with larger targets but reasoning was extrapolative (DXY correlation claims without citing specific historical episodes)
improvements: Add cache staleness threshold — force refresh if news cache exceeds 4h for an active position

Implement contradictory data auto-flagging: when two indicators in the same report conflict (OBV slope vs vol_trend, hash rate current vs trend), force the analyst to explicitly resolve or discount one before scoring
Risk Judge scored event risk 5/5 for CPI but FM had to independently note the Sunday-to-CPI timing overlap — this liquidity-event interaction should be a first-class risk dimension
Bull analyst's self-awareness about past losses was valuable framing — consider making prior-trade-review a required section in debate prompts to reduce recency bias
summary: Solid HOLD decision on an existing short position with coherent reasoning across the chain. The bear case was well-supported by macro (DXY 98.86, S&P -2.8%, risk-off rotation) and derivatives data (persistent negative funding). Data quality was slightly degraded on fundamentals (hash rate contradictions, volume anomaly) and news (stale cache), but analysts correctly flagged issues rather than hiding them. The FM added genuine value by identifying the Sunday-liquidity-to-CPI-event timing risk that the earlier nodes missed.
